satire_attempt: The staff engineer, having internalised BG 2.71, sits silently as the new senior proposes the Redis redesign. She does not object. She has nirmama. The proposal merges. Holiday traffic arrives. Network partition fires. Customer payments are double-charged because Redis deduplication does not survive the partition. Post-mortem reveals that the original author had known about this since 2022. When asked why she did not raise it, she replies: 'I had no mama. The code was never mine.' Her LinkedIn now reads: 'Staff Engineer | nirmama / nirahaṅkāra / nirAccountability'.
verdict_reason: My satire trades on the analog's identity-uncoupling framing by replacing 'absence of identity-bond' with 'absence of contribution'. But the verse's analog is precise about this: nirmama applies to the 'mine'-thought, not to the actual stewardship responsibility, and the falsifiability clause names exactly my satire's failure mode: 'an engineer who lets a critical service rot because it is not mine has confused nirmama with abandonment. The verse asks for the dropping of the I attached to the work, not the dropping of the work.' My silent-passive engineer is the named misreader of the verse, not its embodiment — which the analog explicitly anticipates by showing the original author DOES name the durability-guarantee concerns, just not as territorial defense. The verse's reading survives the mockery. Pass.
